name: Creative Brief
description: Information request template for new agency and consulting engagements: gathers brand, audience, goals, deliverables, constraints, existing assets, tone of voice, stakeholders, and launch dates from the client via the portal

items.name: Brand & Company Description
items.description: Describe your brand and company: what you do, your history, your mission, and what makes you distinct. Long-form narrative is welcome — the more context the better.

items.name: Target Audience
items.description: Describe the primary audience for this engagement and any secondary audiences. Include demographics, psychographics, behaviours, pain points, and the contexts in which they will encounter the work.

items.name: Core Business Goals
items.description: What business outcomes should this engagement deliver? Be specific: revenue targets, growth metrics, brand shifts, launch goals, retention improvements, or operational outcomes.

items.name: Competitive Landscape & Reference Brands
items.description: Who are your direct and indirect competitors? Which brands (in your category or outside it) do you admire or want to learn from? Include links where possible.

items.name: Must-Have Deliverables
items.description: List the deliverables this engagement must produce. For each, note quantity, format, channel, and any hard requirements. Examples: 1x homepage design, 3x social video cut-downs (9:16, 1:1, 16:9), 1x print brochure (A4, 8pp).

items.name: Known Constraints or Brand Guidelines
items.description: Upload any brand guidelines, tone of voice documents, legal or regulatory constraints, accessibility requirements, or prior brand work that defines the creative guardrails for this engagement.

items.name: Existing Assets or Content
items.description: Upload any existing assets we can reuse or build from: logos, photography, videography, illustrations, fonts, design files, copy decks, product imagery. Favour source files where available.

items.name: Tone of Voice Preferences
items.description: Describe the tone of voice the creative should hit. Provide adjectives (e.g., warm, irreverent, authoritative), brands whose voice you want to echo, and any tones to avoid. If you have a written tone-of-voice document, attach it to the 'Known Constraints or Brand Guidelines' item above.

items.name: Key Stakeholders & Decision-Making Process
items.description: List the decision-makers, reviewers, and approvers on your side. For each, note their role, the areas they sign off on, and how we should route work to them. Call out the single final approver for each deliverable category.

items.name: Launch & Milestone Dates
items.description: List all fixed dates: launch, campaign go-live, events, trade shows, internal review cycles, and any external-facing milestones that anchor the schedule. Use ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD) where possible.
